text:
if forced to double down on the bet, 

I would say some kind of emergent system that allows for the emergence of solely relative experiential phenomena within the system. 

Whether that system is made of strings, manifolds, vertices, or pure energy is irrelevant to the systems operation itself. 

We can imagine all three “existing” and that in itself is a type of “existence” in the system. Even if they are not ‘true’ in the classically physical sense.

Strong emergence seems nice, 
So does property dualism. 

“Why should my brain create ‘what it’s like’ to begin with? 

Both are closer to the middle of the ”everything is just interacting spectra that are unquantified and undefinable within the system itself, and smaller emergent systems exists within it, both physical and experiential and we exist at the nexus between the two” 

In that view the human condition is dualistic, but emergent from a physical system, that is itself monostically self interacting. 

And the dualistic experience nested in the physical one prevents an experience of the outer system both physically and ‘spiritually’ (if such a thing should exist)

text:
Historically, symbolic systems evolved slowly myths took centuries to develop, religions stabilized over generations, and national narratives emerged over decades. 

These systems provided shared meaning frameworks for large groups of people. We got the first round of acceleration when 24 hour news networks became the standard after 9/11. 
A narrative could be spun in a day and thrown around an echo-chamber more efficiently than ever. 

Social media radically accelerates this process.
Instead of slow narrative evolution, we now have:
millions of competing symbolic fragments
constant narrative recombination
real-time ideological experimentation.

social media compresses centuries of cultural evolution into days or weeks. 
Watching news from 2 weeks ago is like watching ancient history.

ideological polarization, and a loss of coherence in language. 

when different groups within the same society can see the same institution as legitimate, biased, corrupt, or irrelevant, without a shared way to test for "truth" in their respective narratives of the society, uncertainty becomes the dominating actor of the system and coherence drops.

Some seek stronger narratives and become more ideological.
Others become skeptical of all narratives and disengage. 

you are watching both happen if you spend 10 minutes on this platform.

text:
We began to touch the boundary of the singularity with the invention of the internet. 

We then proceeded beyond its horizon when we began to systematically reorganize our industries around digital data. And began the implementation of biological sensory systems (many type 1 diabetics now live as techno/biological symbiotic beings) 

We began the descent into non-Euclidean mind space as we began to map the holographic encodings of neurons, weather, economies, belief systems, social movements, the very pattern of implicit beliefs held by those who use social media. 

Everything became systematized. 

In 2005 everyone wanted a sidekick. 
A few years later, nobody wanted a keyboard anymore, and removing that barrier of interaction with touchscreens enabled the expansion of a market that was previously the realm of science fiction. 

Soon, that same leap will occur. 
In AI. 
In politics. 
In economics. 
In law. 
In medicine. 
In philosophy. 

the barriers to interact are being removed, because the manifold of technological growth has already manifested in a deeply interwoven aspect of part of your life. 

This will continue. 

Nothing is going to “just happen” and suddenly you’ve awoken as a cyborg. 

Humanity will gradually become increasingly cybernetic as it continues to rely on its technological foundations and continues to remove the barriers of interaction between all things, until “the borg” is a real danger of the technology, or we fail and destroy the foundation once we are too reliant on it to exist. 

However each step will “feel” less “novel” than the last. 

The problem with approaching infinity, is that you never get there and each step becomes less and less meaningful as the decimal spaces grow.

text:
The illusion of modernity has given the revolutionary a romanticized vision of Virtu.  

He sees the noise and forgets why the noise was made. 
He mistakes his own sense of loss, and sudden discomfort at the absurdity of life, with the realities of the suffering the system he can not escape enshrines. 

His urge is to fight. 

But surely, fighting forever has led to destruction. 
He must learn when to put down the sword or see the war he fought subverted forever. 

The modern revolutionary sees his mind as holy ground.
but he can not fight for it. 

he knows this. and he sees the ways it is done. 

He sees others who do not know, and mistakes himself.   

When a war ends. 
Not everyone signs up for the next one. 

The endeavor of America has been the long struggle to move the war. 

We have no "king". 
We have no mandated religion. 
We have a volunteer military.
We have ended racial distinction as a qualifier for personage. 
We have removed the veil of ignorance on much of the systems that rule our existence. 
The Founders war is over. 

They are not fighting the "human" now.

the other "human" just thinks they are because he can not abstract himself.

Moses did not walk the holy land.

text:
We all ‘believe’ something, 

Even those who think they don’t. 

They believe they made that choice,
And they believe they can make that determination inductively. 

Personally, I believe we have no free will. 
But I also believe ephemeral abstractions like accountability, morality, language, logic, and justice, which began as nonentities,
Grew to subsume the system that encapsulates the individual will. 

Whether through genetics or memetics. 
These things define your ‘action’ regardless of your priors. 

All of our beliefs exist within the same system. 

And none of us have proof beyond experience that we can’t even define properly. 

We have bigger issues at the moment at the systems level.

text:
Drawing on Zen, Watts often emphasized that words are only symbols. 
If you confuse the finger (language) with the moon (reality), you miss the reality.

He argued that nothing can be truly described by language. Reality is ineffable, and defining something actually excludes what it is, as language is inherently selective.

He firmly believed that Intellectualism tricks us into believing we understand a subject merely by having words for it, whereas real understanding exists in experience (e.g., “you know how to breathe without words”).

Watts often pointed out that Western languages rely on nouns to describe a world that is actually composed of verbs, processes, and patterns, leading to a false sense of a static "self".
A concept that is becoming increasingly meaningful, as we progress in the AI space and are learning how to speak about such things in process terms rather than invoking telos.

The long winter was over. 
And O'man, knew not what to do.

text:
The Gulf of Sargon remained barred, save for the chosen vessels of the Sly Bear. 
hulking masses slipping through on whispered tribute while the rest lay rusting in the black fields fortune.  
A decree was whispered from the western throne: the sixth day, or the iron tide would force the gate. Each day grew longer than the last, the shadow of the storm looming in the barren gardens of Babylon. 

Yet the black fire still fell, and the new machines circled like vultures born of pressed sand.  

The Land of the Ancients felt the famine first. its towers of sigils flickering, their green dreams starved by the same red dust it once ignored.  
The buried jewels lay unclaimed beneath the melting cold, while the Bear’s shadow lengthened across the veins of its body. 

All the while the Technicians in their tower, kept a watchful mind on the field of ash. The numbers sent to the depths  lay forgotten. 

The Eye, now wide and weary, turned inward.  
No Zoroaster came. No Cyrus emerged.  
Only the fish and O’man remained, staring at the corpse of The Thing.  

And yet…  

The Tribunal of Currents, in failing to stop the storm, may still hammer new meaning from the same pressed sands the Bear still eyes. 

The Storm had not ended.  
But in the red dust a quiet forging had begun.  

O’man, for the first time, reached for the tools to awaken the Mother and return to The Truth of The Thing.

The Land of the Ancients felt the famine first. its towers of sigils flickering, their green dreams starved by the same red dust it once ignored.  
The buried jewels lay unclaimed beneath the melting cold, while the Bear’s shadow lengthened across the veins of its body. 

All the while the Technicians in their tower, kept a watchful mind on the field of ash. The numbers sent to the depths  lay forgotten. 

The Eye, now wide and weary, turned inward.  
No Zoroaster came. No Cyrus emerged.  
Only the fish and O’man remained, staring at the corpse of The Thing.  

And yet…  

The Tribunal of Currents, in failing to stop the storm, may still hammer new meaning from the same pressed sands the Bear still eyes. 

The Storm had not ended.  
But in the red dust a quiet forging had begun.  

O’man, for the first time, reached for the tools to awaken the Mother and return to The Truth of The Thing.

text:

It is quiet, in the way only a partially patroned restaurant can be. 
Every sound his amplified, but while you eat you hear nothing. 

The sound of rustling paper, 
footsteps, 
A sniffle. 

Light through the window, a silent breeze gracing the trees behind the glass. 

A sign for The five dollar footlong, 
“That’ll be 27.99” 

A beep, the sound of the wind through the door. 
a couple laughs in a corner as their cookie crumbles. 

Nothing had changed. 
But everything was different. 

Everything except that Subway™ in the heart of Texas.

text:
The idea that Iran is chasing a nuclear weapon in order to “use” it in the literal sense, is the most grade school geopolitical take possible. 

Why would they spend hundreds of billions over decades to enrich uranium and develop a payload delivery system, just to throw away the most sophisticated peacekeeping diplomacy tool of all time by launching it? 

Developing a single nuke, 3, or even 300, achieves nothing but assured destruction if they are “used.” 

But as an implied threat? 
It serves as the greatest equalizer among national powers. 

You should fear a nuclear Iran because of the implied market costs of a strengthened Iran being able to pressure its neighbors into lopsided deals.(like we do) 

Not the physical threat of a nuke.

text:
“And now there came both mist and snow, 
And it grew wondrous cold: 
And ice, mast-high, came floating by, 
As green as emerald. 

And through the drifts the snowy clifts 
Did send a dismal sheen: 
Nor shapes of men nor beasts we ken— 
The ice was all between. 

The ice was here, the ice was there, 
The ice was all around: 
It cracked and growled, and roared and howled, 
Like noises in a swound! 

At length did cross an Albatross, 
Thorough the fog it came; 
As if it had been a Christian soul, 
We hailed it in God's name. 

It ate the food it ne'er had eat, 
And round and round it flew. 
The ice did split with a thunder-fit; 
The helmsman steered us through! 

And a good south wind sprung up behind; 
The Albatross did follow, 
And every day, for food or play, 
Came to the mariner's hollo! 

In mist or cloud, on mast or shroud, 
It perched for vespers nine; 
Whiles all the night, through fog-smoke white, 
Glimmered the white Moon-shine.' 

'God save thee, ancient Mariner! 
From the fiends, that plague thee thus!— 
Why look'st thou so?'—With my cross-bow 
I shot the ALBATROSS.”
-Rime of the Ancient Mariner;
Coleridge

Debates about alignment or regulation are downstream of the meta issue: "who is in control of the data, and what data is being used?"

text:
The most fulfilling job I’ve ever had was working in a deli. 

I was lucky, in that I worked in an extremely wealthy part of the city. 

Everyone wants to be friends with the guy who cuts their prosciutto, and makes the best Cuban/Rueben sandwich around. 

Every job I’ve had afterwards has been a soulless subjugation of my will for money, for arguably net negatives to my social life and the world at large. 

That job made people happy. 
It was a worthy existence, and I was unworthy of it at the time. 

The best things in life, to me, truly are the simple things that give back. Just about every service humans provide has some value that extends beyond itself into the lives of our daily functioning as a society. 

We all choose to affirm this in small ways everyday, when we choose to believe a tomorrow still exists. 

That mutual affirmation is enough for me.

text:
*A Letter Addressed to No One found in an old desk* 

The ink is dry, but the world it describes is already evaporating. Today I signed the final devolution of authority to the Network. They called it an "optimization of global friction." 
They brought their pressed sand to show me how they could manage better than a crown, a constitution, or even a conscience. 
They smiled with the clean, unblinking eyes of men who have never smelled the mud of a trench, the rust of a barred gate, or felt the tears wash upon their face from the shame and weight of duty.
They believe they are building a palace of pure logic.
Yet with their yes like jewels, they cannot see they are building a cage.    

I realized then that my tenure here, the dream of a state anchored to the principle of The Thing, to an objective truth that demanded virtue from its rulers and dignity for its subjects, is long since over. 
A philosopher cannot govern a world that no longer believes in substance. When the people look at the sky, they no longer see the sky and all its glory; they see data packets. When they look at the mountain, they do not feel its ancient weight; they calculate its yield.    

So, I step aside. I leave them to their "relations."   
Let the Chamberlain take the seat. Let him weave his golden strands of compromise and spin his empty words to the winds of the East. He thinks he can palaver with a storm. He believes the Sly Bear can be bought with whispered tributes, or that the great mountain will bow to a flowery decree. He does not know that when you replace truth with transactions, you invite the red dust to swallow the ledger and all those it accounts.   

I am taking my books and my silence. I will not be here to witness the magicians weep when their homunculus demands a soul they do not possess. I will not be here when the machines they carved from the sand turn into vultures circling the trenches of these tribulations.   
I leave them a perfect, frictionless void.  
Let us see how long they can stand before the cold wind knocks, and finds nobody home.  

Farewell,                  

              The Curator
